reverseString:
/* REXX String Reversal using built-in function */

original_string = "REXX Scripting Example"
SAY 'Original:' original_string

/* Use the REVERSE() built-in function */
reversed_string = REVERSE(original_string)
SAY 'Reversed:' reversed_string

/* Handles typical character sets, behavior with complex Unicode depends on interpreter/platform */
original_unicode = '你好 REXX' /* May depend on execution environment encoding */
reversed_unicode = REVERSE(original_unicode)
SAY 'Original Unicode:' original_unicode
SAY 'Reversed Unicode:' reversed_unicode /* Correctness depends on environment */

EXIT
fibonacci:
/* Calculate Nth Fibonacci number (0-indexed) iteratively */

ARG n . /* Get N from command line argument, default to 10 if none */
IF n = '' THEN n = 10

IF \datatype(n, 'W') | n < 0 THEN DO /* Check if not whole number or negative */
  SAY 'Error: Input must be a non-negative integer.'
  EXIT 1
END

fib_result = fibonacci(n)
SAY 'Fibonacci number F('n') is:' fib_result
EXIT

fibonacci: PROCEDURE /* Define function/procedure */
  ARG num
  IF num = 0 THEN RETURN 0
  IF num = 1 THEN RETURN 1

  a = 0
  b = 1
  DO i = 2 TO num
     /* REXX numbers can be large strings, arithmetic usually handles them */
     next = a + b
     a = b
     b = next
     /* Check for extreme length if necessary, depends on interpreter limits */
     /* IF LENGTH(b) > 50 THEN ... */
  END
  RETURN b
factorial:
/* Calculate Factorial N! iteratively */

ARG n . /* Get N from command line argument, default to 5 if none */
IF n = '' THEN n = 5

IF \datatype(n, 'W') | n < 0 THEN DO /* Check if not whole number or negative */
  SAY 'Error: Input must be a non-negative integer.'
  EXIT 1
END

fact_result = factorial(n)
SAY 'Factorial of' n 'is:' fact_result
EXIT

factorial: PROCEDURE
  ARG num
  result = 1 /* Factorial of 0 is 1 */
  IF num > 0 THEN DO
    DO i = 2 TO num
      result = result * i
      /* REXX numbers are strings; check length for practical limits */
      /* IF LENGTH(result) > 100 THEN SAY 'Warning: Result getting very large' */
    END
  END
  RETURN result
primeCheck:
/* Check if a number is prime */

ARG n . /* Get N from command line argument, default to 29 if none */
IF n = '' THEN n = 29

IF \datatype(n, 'W') | n < 0 THEN DO /* Check if not whole number or negative */
  SAY 'Error: Input must be an integer.'
  EXIT 1
END

IF is_prime(n) THEN
    SAY n 'is prime.'
ELSE
    SAY n 'is not prime.'
EXIT

is_prime: PROCEDURE
  ARG num
  IF num <= 1 THEN RETURN 0 /* False */
  IF num <= 3 THEN RETURN 1 /* True */
  /* Use // for remainder operation */
  IF num // 2 = 0 | num // 3 = 0 THEN RETURN 0 /* False */

  /* Check factors from 5 upwards, stepping by 6 */
  limit = TRUNC(num**0.5) /* Integer square root (TRUNC is common, SQRT depends) */
  i = 5
  DO WHILE i <= limit
     IF num // i = 0 | num // (i + 2) = 0 THEN
        RETURN 0 /* False, divisor found */
     i = i + 6
  END
  RETURN 1 /* True, no divisors found */
readFile:
/* Read content from a file line by line */

ARG file_path . /* Get file path from arguments */
IF file_path = '' THEN file_path = 'rexx_read_test.txt'

/* Create a dummy file for reading */
dummy_content.1 = 'Line 1 content from REXX.'
dummy_content.2 = 'This is the second line.'
dummy_content.3 = 'End.'
dummy_count = 3
CALL LINEOUT file_path, '', 1 /* Overwrite/create */
DO i = 1 TO dummy_count
  CALL LINEOUT file_path, dummy_content.i
END
CALL LINEOUT file_path /* Close the file */
SAY "Created dummy file '"file_path"' for reading."

SAY "\n--- Reading file '"file_path"' line by line ---"

/* Check if file exists and can be read (Regina/ooRexx specific query) */
IF stream(file_path, 'C', 'QUERY EXISTS') = '' THEN DO
    SAY "Error: File not found or cannot be accessed: '"file_path"'"
END
ELSE DO
    line_num = 0
    /* Loop while lines are available */
    DO WHILE LINES(file_path) > 0 /* Check if more lines exist */
        line = LINEIN(file_path) /* Read one line */
        line_num = line_num + 1
        SAY 'Line' line_num':' line
    END
    CALL LINEOUT file_path /* Close the file after reading */
    SAY '--- End of line-by-line read ---
END

/* Clean up dummy file */
rc = stream(file_path, 'C', 'DELETE') /* Regina/ooRexx stream command */
IF rc = 0 THEN
  SAY "\nCleaned up dummy file '"file_path"'."
ELSE
  SAY "\nWarning: Could not delete dummy file '"file_path"'."

EXIT
writeFile:
/* Write or append text content to a file */

ARG file_path .
IF file_path = '' THEN file_path = 'output_rexx.txt'

initial_lines.1 = 'First line written by REXX.'
initial_lines.2 = 'Second line using LINEOUT.'
initial_lines.0 = 2 /* Store count in stem.0 */

line_to_append = 'Third line, appended.'

/* --- Write using LINEOUT with line number 1 (overwrites/creates) --- */
SAY "--- Writing to '"file_path"' (overwrite) ---"
CALL LINEOUT file_path, initial_lines.1, 1 /* Start writing at line 1 (overwrite) */
IF initial_lines.0 > 1 THEN DO /* Write subsequent lines */
  DO i = 2 TO initial_lines.0
     CALL LINEOUT file_path, initial_lines.i
  END
END
CALL LINEOUT file_path /* Close the file */
SAY 'Successfully wrote initial content.'


/* --- Append by opening without line number or using STREAM --- */
SAY "\n--- Appending to '"file_path"' ---"
/* LINEOUT without line number appends after the last write position */
/* Ensure file is positioned correctly or just closed/reopened implicitly */
CALL LINEOUT file_path, line_to_append /* Append line */
CALL LINEOUT file_path /* Close file again */
SAY 'Successfully appended content.'


/* --- Display final content (by reading back) --- */
SAY "\n--- Final content of '"file_path"': ---"
IF stream(file_path, 'C', 'QUERY EXISTS') \= '' THEN DO
    DO WHILE LINES(file_path) > 0
        SAY LINEIN(file_path)
    END
    CALL LINEOUT file_path /* Close */
END
ELSE DO
    SAY "File '"file_path"' not found for display."
END

/* Optional: Clean up */
/* stream(file_path, 'C', 'DELETE') */

EXIT
maxInArray:
/* Find the maximum value in a REXX stem variable (array) */

/* Setup: Populate a stem variable */
numbers.1 = 10
numbers.2 = 5
numbers.3 = 42
numbers.4 = 17
numbers.5 = 8
numbers.6 = 99
numbers.7 = 32
numbers.8 = -5 /* REXX handles signs */
numbers.9 = 0
numbers.0 = 9 /* Store the number of elements in stem.0 */

SAY 'Array:'
DO i = 1 TO numbers.0
  SAY 'numbers.'i '=' numbers.i
END

/* Find the maximum */
IF numbers.0 = 0 THEN DO
    SAY 'Array is empty, cannot find maximum.'
    EXIT
END

current_max = numbers.1 /* Initialize with the first element */

IF numbers.0 > 1 THEN DO
    DO i = 2 TO numbers.0
        /* Standard comparison works for numbers (even as strings) */
        IF numbers.i > current_max THEN
            current_max = numbers.i
    END
END

SAY '\nMaximum value:' current_max

/* Example with strings (lexicographical comparison) */
strings.1 = 'apple'
strings.2 = 'zebra'
strings.3 = 'banana'
strings.0 = 3
max_string = strings.1
DO i = 2 TO strings.0
   IF strings.i > max_string THEN max_string = strings.i
END
SAY '\nMaximum string (lexicographical):' max_string


EXIT
